what_it_does: Defines a browser-global cvAIPresets service that stores AI image presets and image-processing pipelines in localStorage, seeds default records, exposes CRUD/list/find/snapshot/subscribe methods, and materializes presets into generate-image options.
observations: The file is wrapped in an IIFE and attaches its API to window.cvAIPresets.
Storage uses the localStorage key cvstudio:cvAIPresets:v1.
Seed data includes six presets with prompt bodies, model, size, quality, format, background, moderation, and count fields.
Seed data includes two pipelines made of generate, edit, and adopt steps.
The service supports observable updates through a Set of listener functions.
snapshot returns a deep JSON clone, while listPresets and listPipelines return shallow array copies.
materialize maps a preset body to prompt and passes through image generation option fields.
standouts: The file treats prompts, model settings, and output settings as reusable preset records.
Pipeline records describe ordered AI image workflow steps as local data.
The API combines persistence, mutation, subscription, and option materialization in one file-local service.
conventions: Uses a namespaced localStorage key with an explicit version suffix.
Uses seed constants for first-run defaults.
Uses defensive try/catch around localStorage and JSON parsing operations.
Uses object spread for record creation and patch updates.
Exposes a singleton service on window rather than using module exports.
concerns: Pipeline header comments list generate, edit, mask, and filter kinds, but seeded pipelines also use adopt.
Loaded data is accepted without schema validation beyond falling back missing top-level collections.
persist notifies listeners with the mutable data object rather than a clone.
uid uses Math.random and does not check for collisions.
removePreset can delete a preset that a pipeline step may still reference.
notes: This is the localStorage-backed registry and observable API for AI Studio presets and saved image workflows.
questions: Should adopt be documented as a pipeline step kind?
Should loaded preset and pipeline records be validated or migrated by version?
Should removing a preset also handle pipeline steps that reference that preset?
Should subscribers receive cloned snapshots instead of the mutable internal data object?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it stores observable presets and pipelines in localStorage and exposes them for UI use; the comment's listed pipeline kinds are incomplete relative to seeded adopt steps.
intention_signals: The file appears intended to give AI Studio users immediate reusable image-generation presets and simple saved workflows while keeping the data editable and persistent in the browser.
novelty: Pipelines combine image generation/editing steps with adoption targets and tags as persisted workflow data.
contribution: A local, observable preset and pipeline registry for AI image-generation workflows, including seeded architectural visual prompts and a boundary that converts preset records into GenerateOptions.
summary_for_embedding: Browser-local cvAIPresets service for seeded and user-managed AI image presets and ordered image workflow pipelines with localStorage persistence and subscriptions.
intention_for_embedding: This was for giving AI Studio reusable prompt/settings presets and saved image-generation pipelines that the UI can list, mutate, observe, and run.

symbols.does: Loads preset and pipeline data from localStorage or returns seeded defaults.
symbols.description: load reads the namespaced localStorage entry, parses JSON when present, and returns presets and pipelines with collection-level fallback to SEED. If storage is absent or parsing fails, it returns a JSON-cloned copy of the seed data.

symbols.does: Writes the current data object to localStorage and notifies subscribers.
symbols.description: persist stringifies the supplied data into the configured localStorage namespace inside a silent try/catch. After the storage attempt, it iterates registered listeners and invokes each with the same data object, also swallowing listener errors.

symbols.does: Creates a short prefixed identifier using Math.random.
symbols.description: uid concatenates the supplied prefix, an underscore, and a seven-character base36 slice from Math.random. It is used when adding new preset and pipeline records.
